Federal Benefits

Federal Employee Benefits

FERS done right. By people who actually know the system.

Pension timing, TSP strategy, FEHB vs. Medicare, survivor elections, and the mistakes that cost federal employees hundreds of thousands in retirement.

SDBA

Self-Directed Brokerage Accounts

Take control of the money in your 401(k).

If your employer plan offers an SDBA window, you can move beyond the default fund menu into a professionally managed portfolio — without leaving your plan.

★ Live Online Masterclass + Open Q&A with Ann

Complimentary · 60-minute session · No products sold

One hour that changes thirty years of your federal retirement.

FERS, TSP, FEHB, FEGLI, Social Security — five intricate systems, one retirement, and a window of decisions you only get to make once. Join Ann Werts live from anywhere in the country and walk out with the math done.

Thursday, May 14 · 6:00–7:00 PM MT
Live online via Zoom Webinars · No Zoom account required

Open to federal employees nationwide
All federal agencies welcome
What you walk out knowing

Not an agenda. Decisions.

Other workshops read you the OPM handbook. We don't have time for that — and neither do you. Each block ends with a specific decision you'll know how to make by the end of the night.

I
FERS · Pension Timing

Whether to take the FERS supplement at 57 — or wait.

MRA + 10 vs. immediate retirement, the supplement earnings test, the survivor election (and why it's irrevocable on the day you sign).

→ Walk out knowing your earliest unreduced retirement date.
II
TSP · Withdrawal Sequence

Whether to leave money at TSP — or roll out for flexibility.

The G Fund argument, the Mutual Fund Window, the order of withdrawals across TSP, IRA, and taxable, and how Roth fits in.

→ Walk out with a sequence, not a "we'll figure it out."
III
FEHB & Medicare

Whether you actually need Part B at 65 — or just FEHB.

The 5-year rule, premium-sharing math, IRMAA brackets, and what changes if your spouse has separate coverage.

→ Walk out knowing the right Medicare election for your case.
IV
FEGLI & Social Security

Whether FEGLI is still worth it past 65 — and when to claim SS.

The post-65 cost cliff on Option B, when private term wins, plus claiming windows, WEP/GPO, and integrating with your FERS pension.

→ Walk out with a claiming age and a coverage plan.
From the workshop floor

"We had two months until retirement and no idea whether to take the supplement or wait. By the end of the session we knew the answer — and the math behind it. My wife joined from the same screen, and we both walked away lighter."

Federal Retiree · 32 yrs of service · attended online

Your Workshop Instructor

Ann Werts

Federal Benefits Specialist · Capital Wealth

"FERS, TSP, FEHB, Medicare — none of these decisions stand alone. We line them up against your tax bracket, your spouse's coverage, and your timeline. Then we write it down."

Tech check · Joining

Join from anywhere.

Just need a browser and a quiet room. Headphones are nice. We'll cover slides, run the math live on screen, and take questions in the chat. A summary goes out within 48 hours to all attendees.

Zoom · Live · 6:00 PM MT Reserve your seat →
Frequently Asked

Quick clarifications.

Q1Is this affiliated with OPM or my agency?

No. This is independent fiduciary education — not limited by handbook scripts and not endorsed by any federal agency.

Q2Will I be sold financial products?

No products are offered or sold during the workshop. Period. If you want a one-on-one conversation afterward, that's your call.

Q3Can my spouse join?

Please — we encourage it. Watch together from one device, or register separately for your own join link. Survivor elections, FEHB, and SS claiming are joint decisions, not solo ones.

Q4I'm a 6c special-category employee (LEO, FF, ATC). Will the math apply to me?

Yes — we'll cover where 6c rules diverge from standard FERS, including the mandatory retirement age and the supplement difference.

★ ★ ★ Reserve Your Seat ★ ★ ★

One hour. Five systems. One retirement.

Limited seating. The math works the same whether you attend or not — but it's a lot easier to read once someone has done it for you.

Confirm Registration →
Important: Capital Wealth is not affiliated with or endorsed by the U.S. Government, Social Security Administration, Office of Personnel Management, or any federal agency. This is an independent educational webinar. All information presented is for educational purposes only and should not be considered official government guidance.
